canvas.note: Anchors the four-step surface ramp; higher surfaces are lighter, never brighter-hued.

display.purpose: Zilla Slab; screen titles and the brand wordmark. The western/Texas wink.

title.purpose: Zilla Slab; modal and panel titles.

body.purpose: Hanken Grotesk; the UI base — labels, descriptions, task titles.

label.purpose: JetBrains Mono, uppercase; column headers, meta lines, section eyebrows. Floored at 11px.

data.purpose: JetBrains Mono, tabular; the signature stopwatch/day-total readout, and every inline duration/clock/total.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: Gentle rest-state lift for cards, chips, small raised elements. Light theme: 0 2px 8px rgba(20,22,27,0.08).

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Dropdowns, suggestion popovers, toasts, the right-hand task drawer. Light theme: 0.16 alpha.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Centered dialogs over the 62%-black scrim. Deepest layer. Light theme: 0.22 alpha.

shadows.name: live-halo
shadows.purpose: The pulsing accent halo on the live timer dot. Reserved for live state, never decorative.

motion.name: pulse
motion.purpose: The running-timer heartbeat — live dot and live grid cell.

motion.name: quick-fade
motion.purpose: Hover-reveal affordances (per-day add buttons, quiet controls).

breakpoints.name: phone
breakpoints.purpose: Sidebar -> bottom tab bar; grid table -> stacked day-cards. The single breakpoint (desktop-first).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid accent call-to-action (Start, Save). Brightens on hover, no color swap.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Quiet secondary action on a raised surface.

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Modal/settings field on the deepest canvas surface; accent border on focus.

components.name: Activity Chip
components.description: Selectable activity/catalog chip; mono, pill-shaped, accent tint on hover.

components.name: Uncategorized Pill
components.description: The amber 'needs completing' call-to-action on an uncategorized entry.

components.name: Catalog Card
components.description: A code catalog card: panel surface, hairline border, ambient rest shadow.

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item (active)
components.description: Active navigation row: top-of-ramp surface, semibold, high-contrast text.
components.html: <button class="ds-nav-item"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="18" height="18" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="9"/><path d="M12 7v5l3 2"/></svg>Tracker</button>

components.name: Timer Readout (signature)
components.description: The running stopwatch — the product heartbeat: pulsing accent dot + large tabular mono clock.

components.name: Grid Tick Cell (signature)
components.description: A 'done' grid cell: green wash, tabular mono duration, and the at-rest tick checkbox marking a line entered into the Timesheet system.

narrative.overview: Walker is a dark, dense precision instrument for recording real work time against real charge codes. The whole surface reads like a well-kept ledger seen under low console light: rows of tabular monospace numerals, thin ruled lines, entries ticked off one at a time. The name earns a quiet western wink — a five-point star badge, a Zilla Slab slab-serif for titles, microcopy like 'Adios, backlog.' — a deliberate homage to Texas, an earlier in-house timesheet tool this one supersedes. The wink is affectionate, never a costume. The mood is precise and quiet: information first, zero decorative noise, character in the exactness of details rather than ornament. Where it softens it softens gently — muted desaturated color (no pure black, no pure white), translucent state tints, generously rounded containers: a felt-lined ledger, not a steel cockpit. Depth is carried mostly by tonal surface layering, with soft ambient shadows lifting resting cards a touch. Every number the user reads or edits is set in monospace with tabular figures — the single most defining trait: the data speaks in monospace; the interface speaks in sans.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-first (with a fully maintained light theme), muted and desaturated — never pure black or white.
Monospace tabular numerals for all time/duration/quantitative data; sans-serif for UI; slab-serif for titles.
Dense data-grid readability over expressive flourish; the layout mirrors the target Timesheet system's grid.
A quiet Texas/western wink confined to the badge, display type, and copy.
Semantic color is functional and rationed: amber = needs attention, green = done, blue = live/active.

rules.name: The Two-Theme-One-Hue Rule
rules.body: Dark and light themes share the same fonts and accent hue family; only surface/text/border tokens invert. The accent is darkened for AA on light (#2f6fe0), never re-hued. Never introduce a token that exists in one theme but not the other.

rules.name: The Rationed Semantic Rule
rules.body: Amber, green, and red are meanings, not decoration. Amber only says 'needs categorizing/attention,' green only 'entered/done,' red only 'destructive/error.' If a color would be decorative, use a neutral surface.

rules.name: The Catalog-Palette Exception
rules.body: Saturated color belongs to exactly one place: the 64-swatch code palette users assign to Timesheet codes for at-a-glance grid identity. Chrome stays muted; only user-owned code dots carry vivid hue.

rules.name: The Monospace-Data Rule
rules.body: Every quantitative or time value — durations, clock times, HHMM military-time inputs, totals, counts, code numbers — is set in JetBrains Mono with tabular-nums. Prose never borrows the mono; data never borrows the sans.

rules.name: The Slab-For-Identity Rule
rules.body: Zilla Slab appears only on identity and titling (wordmark, screen titles, modal titles, empty-state headlines). It never sets body copy or data.

rules.name: The Live-Ring Rule
rules.body: The one non-shadow glow is the accent focus ring and pulsing halo on the running timer. Reserve halos for live state; never use them decoratively.

dos: Do set every duration, clock time, total, count, and code number in JetBrains Mono with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. Data is monospace; UI is sans.
Do build depth from the surface ramp (canvas -> panel -> raised -> hover) first; add an ambient shadow only for a gentle rest-state lift, and a floating/modal shadow only for things that detach.
Do signal interaction with translucent accent tints (accent-soft fill, accent-line border), not hard color swaps or thickened borders.
Do keep amber/green/red strictly semantic (needs-attention / done / destructive).
Do define any new color token in both dark and light themes, same hue family.
Do floor functional text at 11px and keep text-lo only on surfaces where it clears WCAG AA.
donts: Don't use pure black (#000) or pure white (#fff); the palette is muted and cool by design.
Don't let the Texas/western wink grow past the badge, display type, and microcopy — no full western skin, textures, or ornament that would cost data density.
Don't spend saturated color on chrome; vivid hue is reserved for user-assigned code swatches.

Don't draw progress bars or gauges that enforce an 8h/day or period target — Walker shows real, to-the-minute durations and never editorializes totals (the checklist progress bar, which counts entered lines, is the one allowed progress meter).
Don't introduce a third numeric typeface or set data in the sans; don't set body copy in the slab.
